tools.name: tcpdump
tools.description: Capture and analyze network traffic using tcpdump. To control how long the capture runs, set the 'timeout_seconds' parameter (default 60). Do NOT use -G or -W flags for duration control — they will be ignored by the timeout wrapper. You may use '-c <count>' to limit by packet count, and '-n' to disable DNS resolution.

tools.name: tshark
tools.description: Capture and analyze network traffic using tshark (Wireshark CLI). To control how long the capture runs, set the 'timeout_seconds' parameter (default 60). Do NOT use '-a duration' for duration control. You may use '-c <count>' to limit by packet count.

tools.name: netdiscover
tools.description: Active/passive ARP reconnaissance tool to discover hosts on a network. To control how long the scan runs, set the 'timeout_seconds' parameter (default 60). You may use '-r <range>' to specify a target subnet.

tools.name: arp_scan
tools.description: Send ARP packets to local network to discover alive hosts. CRITICAL: Ensure you specify a strict target like '-l' (local network) or a specific CIDR so it terminates quickly without hanging.

tools.name: nmap
tools.description: Network exploration tool and security / port scanner. CRITICAL: For broad or blind scans, you MUST use fast flags (e.g., '-F', '-T4') or strict timeouts (e.g., '--host-timeout 5m') to avoid the command taking too long and freezing the session.

tools.name: arpspoof
tools.description: Intercept traffic on a switched LAN by forging ARP replies. To control how long the attack runs, set the 'timeout_seconds' parameter (default 60).

tools.name: ettercap
tools.description: Comprehensive suite for man-in-the-middle attacks on LAN. Runs in Text-only mode (-T). To control how long the attack runs, set the 'timeout_seconds' parameter (default 60).

tools.name: rip_request
tools.description: Send RIPv1/v2 Request packets (Scapy) to enumerate the full routing table from RIP-enabled routers, then capture and parse RIP Responses. Requires root. Args: [--iface IFACE] [--target IP] [--version {1,2}] [--timeout SECS] [--send-only]. Defaults: RIPv2 multicast (224.0.0.9), timeout=10s. Use timeout_seconds to cap the overall runtime.

tools.name: ospf_sniff
tools.description: OSPF discovery via tshark (passive) with optional active Hello injection (Scapy) to elicit immediate neighbour responses. Passive mode captures all OSPF message types and LSA types. Active mode (--active) sends a crafted OSPF Hello to 224.0.0.5, prompting any OSPF router on the segment to reply before their timer fires. Requires root and tshark; active mode also requires scapy. Args: [--iface IFACE] [--timeout SECS] [--verbose] [--active] [--router-id ID] [--area AREA] [--src-ip IP] [--netmask MASK]. Default timeout=60s.

tools.name: vtysh
tools.description: Query Quagga/FRRouting routing daemons interactively via vtysh. Useful on a router or host running FRR/Quagga. Common commands: -c 'show ip bgp' (full BGP table), -c 'show bgp summary' (peer summary), -c 'show ip ospf neighbor' (OSPF neighbours), -c 'show ip rip' (RIP routes), -c 'show ip route' (unified routing table). Requires vtysh and local daemon access.

tools.name: exabgp
tools.description: BGP peer manipulation and route injection tool (ExaBGP). Used to establish BGP sessions with a peer, announce or withdraw prefixes, and probe BGP implementations. Typically invoked with a configuration file: exabgp <config.conf>. Can also be used for BGP fuzzing and monitoring. Requires exabgp installed (apt install exabgp).
